id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__a9-hybrid-small-to-big__0034
text: In 2021, people spent on average about [seven hours](https://datareportal.com/reports/digital-2022-october-global-statshot "DIGITAL 2022: OCTOBER GLOBAL STATSHOT REPORT") a day in online channels. Advertisers therefore spend billions a year to show their message to website visitors and app users. This market is growing fast; in 2021 this amount in Europe had risen to [92 billion euros](https://iabeurope.eu/wp-content/uploads/2022/06/adex-2021-advertising-report.pdf "DIGITAL ADVERTISING IN EUROPE") – an increase of 30.5 per cent compared with the previous year. In the Netherlands, digital ad spend exceeded advertising spend in traditional media for the first time in 2016 and by now the digital ad spend is already more than twice as large. For the owners of websites, apps and other content – also known as ‘publishers’ – the internet offers opportunities to monetise their ad space. For example, almost half of the largest websites use a so-called ad network. This is a technological solution that collects online ad space from publishers and offers it to advertisers. Besides advertisers and publishers, there are numerous intermediaries and other service providers active in the online advertising market. Advertisers are supported by media, marketing and advertising agencies, which help determine the most effective channels for online ad campaigns and often also play a role in the associated purchase of ad space. There are also numerous so-called AdTech players, which use technological solutions to facilitate the buying and selling of online ad space. Personalised advertising as standard practice.

metadata.description: The online advertising market is exploring alternatives to personalised ads due to privacy legislation. Read more.

text: AI makes capable attackers even stronger. Generative AI is also used to write malware.

metadata.source_title: Cyberattacks more dangerous due to artificial intelligence
metadata.tags: Rapport, Technologie, Media & Telecom, AI, Cybersecurity, Digitalisering, Sociaal ondernemen
metadata.author: Julia Krauwer
metadata.description: More than half of companies view innovations in artificial intelligence (AI) as a threat to their organisation’s cyber security.
metadata.parent_text: AI makes capable attackers even stronger. Generative AI is also used to write malware.
